zh.lifeSummary: 當Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena於1095年出生在德國海因里希_普洛文次 維斯特法倫阿爾騰亞賀爾謝易德伯格時，他的父親Graf Adolf von Berg III15歲，母親Adelheid von Laufen46歲。他於大約1127年與Irmgard von Schwarzenburg結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他於1160年被列為mönch。他去世於1170年10月12日在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾享年75歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾阿爾騰格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena于1095年出生在德国海因里希_普洛文次 維斯特法倫阿爾騰亞賀爾謝易德伯格时，他的父亲Graf Adolf von Berg III15岁，母亲Adelheid von Laufen46岁。他于大约1127年与Irmgard von Schwarzenburg结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他于1160年被列为mönch。他去世于1170年10月12日在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾享年75岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾阿爾騰格。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Adolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Adelwolf, a compound of adal ‘noble’ + wolf ‘wolf’.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena was born in 1095, in Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Westphalia, Prussia, Germany, his father, Graf Adolf von Berg III, was 15 and his mother, Adelheid von Laufen, was 46. He married Irmgard von Schwarzenburg about 1127. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. In 1160, at the age of 65, his occupation is listed as mönch. He died on 12 October 1170, in Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, at the age of 75, and was buried in Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena est né en 1095, à Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Provinz Westfalen, Prusse, Allemagne, son père, Graf Adolf von Berg III, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Adelheid von Laufen, avait 46 ans. Il a épousé Irmgard von Schwarzenburg environ 1127. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. En 1160, à l’âge de 65 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est mönch. Il est décédé le 12 octobre 1170, à Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena 1095 in Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Graf Adolf von Berg III 15 und seine Mutter Adelheid von Laufen 46. Er hat um 1127 Irmgard von Schwarzenburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. 1160, als er 65 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als mönch aufgeführt. Er starb am 12. Oktober 1170 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland und wurde in Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena è nato il nel 1095 nel Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, Germania, il suo padre, Graf Adolf von Berg III, aveva 15 anni e il suo madre, Adelheid von Laufen, aveva 46 anni. Lui sposato Irmgard von Schwarzenburg circa 1127. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Nel 1160, all'età di 65 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come mönch. Morì il 12 ottobre 1170, nel Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altenaが1095年にドイツプレウssンプロヴィンズ ウエストファレンアルテナハーシェイドバーグで生まれた時，父Graf Adolf von Berg IIIは15歳，母Adelheid von Laufenは46歳であった。約1127年にIrmgard von Schwarzenburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。1160年，65歳の時，職業はmönchと記録されている。彼は1170年10月12日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンルヘイニスチ-バージッシャー クレイスオデンサルで75歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンルヘイニスチ-バージッシャー クレイスオデンサルアルテンバーグにて埋葬された。

ko.Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergische: 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 리이니슈-버지슈어 크레이스 오덴솔 알텐버그
ko.Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Westfalen, Preu: 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 웨스트파렌 알텐아 허슈이드 버그
ko.Burg Berge, Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinis: 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 리이니슈-버지슈어 크레이스 오덴솔 알텐버그
ko.lifeSummary: Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena(이)가 1095년 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 웨스트파렌 알텐아 허슈이드 버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Graf Adolf von Berg III(은)는 15세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelheid von Laufen(은)는 46세였다. 그는 대략 1127년 Irmgard von Schwarzenburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 1160년, 65세의 나이로 그의 직업은 mönch이었다. 그는 1170년 10월 12일 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 리이니슈-버지슈어 크레이스 오덴솔에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 리이니슈-버지슈어 크레이스 오덴솔 알텐버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena nasceu em 1095, em Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, Alemanha, seu pai, Graf Adolf von Berg III, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid von Laufen, tinha 46 anos. Ele casou-se com Irmgard von Schwarzenburg aproximadamente 1127. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Em 1160, com 65 anos, sua profissão aparece como mönch. Ele faleceu em 12 de outubro de 1170, em Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergische: алтенбирг, одентэл, раинищ-биргищир крис, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия
ru.Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Westfalen, Preu: бирг, хирщид, алтена, провинз вестфеилен, Пруссия, Германия
ru.Burg Berge, Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinis: алтенбирг, одентэл, раинищ-биргищир крис, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena родился в 1095 г. в местности бирг, хирщид, алтена, провинз вестфеилен, Пруссия, Германия, его отцу по имени Graf Adolf von Berg III было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Laufen, 46. Он женился на женщине по имени Irmgard von Schwarzenburg около 1127 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. В 1160 году, в возрасте 65 лет,  он работал по профессии mönch. Он умер 12 октября 1170 г. в местности одентэл, раинищ-биргищир крис, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности алтенбирг, одентэл, раинищ-биргищир крис, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Graf Adolf IV von Berg und Altena nació en 1095, en Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, Alemania, su papá, Graf Adolf von Berg III, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Adelheid von Laufen, tenía 46 años. Se casó con Irmgard von Schwarzenburg aproximadamente 1127. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. En 1160, a la edad de 65 años, su ocupación registrada era mönch. Murió el 12 de octubre de 1170, en Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
